item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The End of Death scenario was caused by something else. This is a thing that was recovered before it had happened, and what changed for it after it had happened.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is the end of death scenario a result of 4206? Or is 4206 just used to keep people healthier?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: But we have SCP-392 (or that would be considered cross-test?)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Who needs embalming fluid and autopsies when you have an Anubis mask that does all the work for you!
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel bad for the doggos… It's really tough to read, meaning it's really well done. Then again, only reading the ilnesses the -1 specimens have hit me hard. Good job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Man end of death articles are always so bleak. Loved it tho and loved seeing the ethics committee's change to EOD.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dramps ninja'd me while I was typing it up darn it

why isn't it Thaumiel?

The Object Class Guide describes Thaumeil as such: "Thaumiel-class SCPs are anomalies that the Foundation specifically uses to contain other SCPs." Though I can see how this particular object can be viewed as Thaumiel, and I definitely agree that this anomaly is a useful one, I personally don't think it should be designated as such because it doesn't directly assist in containment. It's a medical tool.

On the same note, if it can be safely stored in a locker, why isn't it Safe?

The mask alone would technically be safe, but the -1 instances require intensive care and feeding, which makes taking care of them a more hands-on affair than average animals, earning it the Euclid title.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's not Thaumiel because it doesn't contain other anomalies. It isn't safe because the wolves are part of the anomaly and they need to be taken care of

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a pretty novel idea, as it offers a reason why MTFs and the like are able to still have members working even after failed operations. However, one thing I can't get past is the object class. If this item is beneficial to the Foundation, why isn't it Thaumiel? On the same note, if it can be safely stored in a locker, why isn't it Safe?

Other than that, I like the idea of this. The Egyptian theme, the resourcefulness of the wolves, the drama between the researchers of the skip with the Ethics committee, I dig it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Neat piece; thanks for sharing it with me! I enjoy the downers of EOD.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks to nico for letting me be co-author. :>

The original draft had been collecting dust in a sandbox for at least 3 years, so it's great to finally see something come out of it. Even if it's a little different from what I originally had in mind.
It was just an ancient Egyptian furry. Don't tell anyone. >:3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Gotta love that grim feeling only EOD delivers

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This article was originally DrAnnoyingDog's, who submitted it for a draft swap we are both participating in. The original draft was very different but what some of the core concepts are still there.

Thanks to manymeats, rtme, kirby, tufto, and everyone else who read this along the way.
